description:
### 1. Overall Layout
The figure is a wide, horizontally oriented taxonomy chart structured as a vertical block diagram, utilizing a clean, modular layout typical of top-tier academic papers. It categorizes eight text compression methods into five functional families across five distinct column groups. A neat legend sits at the top, and a horizontal axis runs along the bottom to categorize the granularity of each method. The composition relies on an implicit grid for perfect alignment, ensuring even visual weight distribution, consistent spacing, and a clear left-to-right progression.

### 2. Groupings (Column Families)
The diagram is divided into five vertical column groups. Each group is visually anchored by a distinct, muted pastel horizontal bar at the very top, acting as a categorical identifier. Immediately below each bar is the column header text, rendered in bold, neutral sans-serif typography:
*   **Column 1 (Far Left):** Topped with a soft sky blue bar. Header: "Truncation".
*   **Column 2:** Topped with a desaturated sage green bar. Header: "Random sampling".
*   **Column 3:** Topped with a warm peach bar. Header: "Extractive graph".
*   **Column 4:** Topped with a dusty lavender bar. Header: "Structured memory".
*   **Column 5 (Far Right):** Topped with a soft brick red bar. Header: "Abstractive".

### 3. Components (Method Boxes and Unit Tags)
Within each column, the specific compression methods are depicted as softened geometric shapes—specifically, rounded rectangles with crisp white fills, thin near-black borders, and a very subtle, flat drop shadow to provide a slight lift from the pure white background. Text inside is centered and highly legible. Below the method boxes in each column sits the "Unit tag," rendered in a slightly smaller, muted dark grey sans-serif font to establish a clear visual hierarchy.
*   **Under Truncation:** Two rounded method boxes labeled "truncate_head" and "truncate_tail". Unit tag below: "token".
*   **Under Random sampling:** One rounded method box labeled "random_sentences". Unit tag below: "sentence".
*   **Under Extractive graph:** Two rounded method boxes labeled "LexRank" and "TextRank". Unit tag below: "sentence".
*   **Under Structured memory:** One wide rounded method box labeled "structured_memory (regex: years, numerics, proper nouns, quoted strings)". Unit tag below: "fact".
*   **Under Abstractive:** Two rounded method boxes labeled "abstractive_bart (single pass)" and "iterative_abstractive (2x budget then resummarise)". Unit tag below: "token (learned)".

### 4. Connections and Bottom Axis Mapping
Relationships and categorizations are established through precise spatial alignment rather than flow arrows.
*   **Horizontal Axis:** A clean, solid dark grey horizontal line spans the bottom of the figure. The axis title "Finest compressible unit" is placed neatly to the left or centered below it in bold sans-serif.
*   **Axis Ticks and Check Marks:** Three tick labels sit along the axis, mapping to the columns above via minimalist, muted green check mark icons.
    *   Tick label "token-prefix": Located under the first column, with a single green check mark aligned directly upward into the "Truncation" column.
    *   Tick label "sentence": Located centrally, with two green check marks aligned directly upward into the "Random sampling" and "Extractive graph" columns.
    *   Tick label "sub-sentence": Located on the right, with two green check marks aligned directly upward into the "Structured memory" and "Abstractive" columns.

### 5. Labels and Annotations
All text is rendered letter-perfect in a clean, neutral sans-serif font (such as Helvetica or Inter), with black text ensuring maximum contrast and legibility against the white background.
*   **Legend:** Positioned cleanly at the top of the diagram. It displays small colored squares matching the top categorical bars, paired with exact text:
    *   [Soft sky blue square] Truncation family (truncate_head, truncate_tail)
    *   [Desaturated sage green square] Random-sampling family (random_sentences)
    *   [Warm peach square] Extractive graph family (LexRank, TextRank)
    *   [Dusty lavender square] Structured memory family (structured_memory)
    *   [Soft brick red square] Abstractive family (abstractive_bart, iterative_abstractive)

### 6. Input/Output
As a taxonomy diagram rather than a system architecture graph, there are no explicit data flow arrows entering or exiting the system. The "input" concept is elegantly abstracted as the "Finest compressible unit" axis at the bottom, which defines the base textual unit each method processes.

### 7. Styling
*   **Background:** Pure white, strictly devoid of any gradients, grids, vignettes, or distracting textures.
*   **Color Palette:** Print-safe, colorblind-aware, and highly muted. Categorical colors are restricted to the five soft pastels used for the top bars and legend, plus the muted green for the check marks.
*   **Shapes and Outlines:** Method boxes feature softened corners, thin and consistent dark charcoal strokes, and subtle flat drop shadows that maintain a clean, academic aesthetic without violating the flat background rule.
*   **Typography:** Black sans-serif text throughout. No 3D bevels, glows, or handwriting fonts. The layout is highly structured, compact, and perfectly balanced for a half-page two-column format.
